claim_results.claim: Genesis genealogies fitted to a quantum decoherence equation predict an asymptotic lifespan floor of 93 years — matching Moses' "70 or 80" (Psalm 90:10), the modern global average, and Jeanne Calment's 122-year ceiling.
We took the Genesis genealogies â€” Adam through Joseph, raw lifespan numbers from Genesis 5 and 11 â€” and fit them to a quantum decoherence equation.
The model was not told about Psalm 90.
Four Independent Sources, One Number: A quantum decoherence equation fitted to Genesis genealogies predicts an asymptotic floor of 93 years.
These two observations define the entire model.
The Floor: Why Lifespans Don't Hit Zero T he model fitted to Genesis genealogies independently predicts a floor of 93 years.
The floor is not theological decoration on a physics model.

â€¢ Model floor (exponential fit): 93 years â€¢ Moses (Psalm 90:10): 70â€“80 years â€¢ Modern global average: 73 years â€¢ Modern developed nations: 78â€“82 years All four sources cluster around 70â€“93 years.
The model doesn't know about Psalm 90 or modern data.
The data shows a sharp discontinuity at the Flood.
A quantum decoherence equation, fitted purely to Genesis genealogies and asked where the curve levels off, says: 93 years.
Modern actuarial data shows a global average of 73 years and 78â€“82 for developed nations.
All four sources â€” the mathematical model, the ancient prophecy, and two independent modern measurements â€” cluster in the same range.
The model wasn't told about the prophecy.
The framework predicts that this convergence is not random.
Any claim that turns a structural resemblance into a proven mechanism belongs here until the math, experiment, or textual chain can carry the weight.
Possible overstatement: language that sounds final should be read as a working model unless the article explicitly proves it.

Executive Summary We fit the Genesis lifespan record (Adam through Moses) to a standard quantum decoherence equation and the curve resolves cleanly: $C(t) = 337\, e^{-t/214} + 93$, with $R^2 = 0.888$ and $p = 4.73\times10^{-7}$.
The model wasn't told about Moses or modern actuarial data; convergence on the same number across four independent sources is measurement, not construction.
Rigor & Kill Conditions Every claim is held to explicit falsification standards.
Kill if: the post-Flood lifespans do not fit an exponential decoherence form, or the asymptote drifts substantially away from the 70–93 year band when the model is re-fit with held-out data.

Kill if: the four-source convergence (model floor, Moses, global average, developed-nation average) is shown to be circular — e.g. if the genealogies were back-edited to Mosaic norms — rather than independent measurements landing on the same number.
The decoherence model behind this article, collected as a single reference.
Plain English: The lifespan asymptote is not zero because grace is a continuous source term.
$$\text{Floor}_{model} \approx \text{Floor}_{Moses} \approx \text{Floor}_{global} \approx \text{Floor}_{developed}$$ Model: 93 yr (decoherence fit).
The model wasn't told about Psalm 90 or modern actuarial data.
Same Master-Equation variable, two coordinate names: "entropy" in the physics frame, "sin" in the theological frame.
5.9% across pre-Flood lifespans â€” extremely low, evidence of stationarity.

Trend Test ($p_{trend}$) Statistical test for whether a slope differs from zero. $p = 0.68$ across pre-Flood data means no significant trend. $R^2$ (Goodness of Fit) Fraction of variance explained by the model.
Model floor, Moses' Psalm 90:10, modern global average, modern developed-nation average â€” all clustering at 70â€“93 years.
